The Crown Jewel

THERE IS NOWHERE ELSE LIKE MONACO

Every year, the streets of Monte Carlo are transformed into the most iconic motor racing circuit on earth. F1 cars thread through the Casino, plunge into the tunnel and burst out into the sunlit harbour — barriers just centimetres away, superyachts lining the waterfront, the Mediterranean shimmering in the background.

Part of the legendary Triple Crown of Motorsport alongside the Indianapolis 500 and Le Mans 24 Hours, the Monaco Grand Prix has been captivating the world since 1929. There is no other race that combines raw motorsport with this level of glamour, history and spectacle.

"Like riding a bicycle around your living room."
— Nelson Piquet on driving Monaco

Years of History (since 1929)

The Monaco Experience

WATCH FROM A
HARBOUR YACHT

There is no better way to experience Monaco than from a luxury yacht moored directly on the circuit. With multiple viewing decks, all-inclusive hospitality and the iconic skyline behind you, this is Monaco at its most glamorous.

Packages available for 1, 2 or 3 days. Friday on a yacht is the most affordable way in — when prices are significantly lower than Saturday and Sunday.

Ultimate access

F1 PADDOCK CLUB™

In Monaco, the Paddock Club is unlike any other circuit. Rather than above the pit lane, the Paddock Club terrace overlooks iconic La Rascasse corner — with the entire harbour as your backdrop. Add the Paddock Club Yacht for the ultimate two-venue experience.

La Rascasse Terrace

Exclusive viewing terrace overlooking La Rascasse corner and the Monaco harbour.

Gourmet Dining

All-inclusive fine dining, champagne, premium wines and beverages throughout the day.

Paddock Access

Guided tours of the paddock and team garage areas. Meet drivers and F1 ambassadors.

Paddock Club Yacht

Combine the terrace with an exclusive moored yacht for the ultimate two-venue Monaco package.

Before you book

GOOD TO KNOW

Buying tickets at Monaco

Unlike other F1 races, Monaco tickets are commonly bought per day — it's normal to be in a different stand or terrace each day. Friday grandstand tickets are cheapest at around €170.

Thursday is free

Thursday entry to the circuit is just €30 and is a relaxed atmosphere with support series action. A great low-cost way to experience the circuit.

Grandstands not covered

Most grandstands at Monaco are open-air. Bring sun protection and check the weather — Monaco in June is typically warm (20–25°C) with sunshine, but rain is possible.

Accommodation tip

Monaco itself is extremely expensive during race week. Most fans stay in Nice (30 mins by train) or Cannes. Book accommodation as early as possible — it sells out months in advance.

Getting there

Nice Airport (NCE) is the nearest major airport — 30 minutes by taxi or helicopter to Monaco. Trains run regularly from Nice to Monaco-Monte-Carlo station, a short walk from the circuit.

Best grandstands

Grandstand K at Tabac corner is the fan favourite — harbour views and great atmosphere. Grandstand T is best for pit lane views. Grandstand B at Sainte-Dévote is great for first-lap drama.

FAQ

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

How is Monaco different to other F1 races?
+
Monaco is the only race that doesn't meet the standard 305km minimum distance — at 260km it's shorter, but the 78 laps through the streets of Monte Carlo take just as long. It's the only race on the calendar with just one DRS zone. Overtaking is extremely difficult, meaning qualifying pole position is crucial — the race has been won from pole 32 times since 1950. Part of the Triple Crown of Motorsport alongside Indianapolis 500 and Le Mans 24 Hours.
Is it better to watch from a grandstand or a yacht?
+
Both are fantastic but completely different experiences. A grandstand gives you raw, trackside atmosphere — the noise, the speed, the crowd around you. A yacht gives you the glamour of Monaco: stunning views, all-inclusive hospitality and the iconic harbour backdrop. Many fans do one day on a yacht (Friday, when prices are much lower) and the rest in a grandstand.
Which grandstand has the best view at Monaco?
+
Grandstand K at Tabac corner is consistently rated the best by fans — it sits right at the harbour and gives stunning panoramic views. Grandstand T at the Swimming Pool section offers pit views. Grandstand B at Sainte-Dévote (Turn 1) is great for the race start and first lap drama. We can advise on the best seats within each stand — just message us.
When were Monaco GP 2026 tickets released?
+
Tickets went on sale in two phases via the Automobile Club de Monaco (ACM): a pre-sale for ACM members on September 8 2025, and general public sales from September 22 2025. Many popular grandstand seats have already sold out. Contact us via WhatsApp to check current availability.
How do I receive my tickets?
+
Tickets are sent electronically after payment is confirmed. You show them on your phone at the circuit entrance — no printing needed. For hospitality and yacht packages, we'll send you a full info pack with meeting points and timings.
